seoTitle: Fill Out Form 1095-A in TurboTax Correctly
metaDescription: Learn how to fill out Form 1095-A in TurboTax to accurately report Health Insurance Marketplace coverage and Premium Tax Credits.
intro: If you got health coverage through the Marketplace, Form 1095-A directly affects your Premium Tax Credit calculation and needs to be entered carefully. This guide walks through how to fill out Form 1095-A in TurboTax step by step. It's aimed at individuals and tax preparers who want to avoid common entry errors on this form.

steps.heading: Locate your Form 1095-A
steps.body: Find your Form 1095-A from the Health Insurance Marketplace before starting entry in TurboTax.

steps.heading: Navigate to the health coverage section
steps.body: Within TurboTax, go to the section for reporting Marketplace health insurance coverage.

steps.heading: Enter coverage and premium data
steps.body: Input the monthly premium amounts, second-lowest cost silver plan figures, and advance payment amounts exactly as shown on your form.

steps.heading: Review for accuracy
steps.body: Double-check each entry against your physical or digital 1095-A to avoid mismatches that could delay your return.

steps.heading: Let TurboTax calculate your credit
steps.body: TurboTax will use the entered data to calculate your Premium Tax Credit or any amount you may owe back.

faq.question: What happens if I enter my 1095-A information incorrectly?
faq.answer: Errors can affect your Premium Tax Credit calculation and may delay processing or require an amended return.

faq.question: Where do I get my Form 1095-A if I lost it?
faq.answer: You can typically retrieve it through your Health Insurance Marketplace account online.

faq.question: Does everyone with health insurance need to file Form 1095-A?
faq.answer: No, only those who obtained coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace receive and need to report this specific form.

faq.question: Can TurboTax calculate my Premium Tax Credit automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once your 1095-A data is entered correctly, TurboTax calculates the credit or repayment amount automatically.
